title: Getting Started with Next.js App Router
excerpt: A practical introduction to routing, layouts, and data flow with App Router.
content:
Next.js App Router helps you compose pages with layouts and nested routes.

In this sample post, we focus on server components, file-based routing, and reusable UI patterns.

author.name: Alex Carter
author.bio: Frontend engineer focused on React and performance.

title: Server Components vs Client Components
excerpt: Learn where each component type fits and how to avoid hydration issues.
content:
Server components are ideal for data-heavy rendering.

Client components handle interaction and local state.

author.name: Maya Lopez
author.bio: Builds developer tooling and teaching materials.

title: Building Dynamic Blog Routes
excerpt: Implement [slug] routes and not-found handling the safe way.
content:
Dynamic segments let you map content by slug.

Always include fallback handling when data is missing.

author.name: Alex Carter
author.bio: Frontend engineer focused on React and performance.

title: Contact Form Validation with Zod
excerpt: Validate user input with shared schemas for both UI and API route.
content:
Schema-driven validation reduces mismatch between frontend and backend.

React Hook Form works well with Zod resolver for consistent errors.

author.name: Ivy Nguyen
author.bio: Teaches frontend architecture and form design.

title: Search and Filter in App Router
excerpt: Keep filters in URL params so users can share and revisit state.
content:
URL-driven search makes state linkable and browser-history friendly.

Debounce input updates to avoid noisy navigation.

author.name: Maya Lopez
author.bio: Builds developer tooling and teaching materials.

title: Data Fetching Patterns for Blog Pages
excerpt: Pick SSR, static generation, or client fetching by page behavior.
content:
Rendering strategy should follow data freshness and interaction needs.

Use server fetching for initial content and client fetching for dynamic interactions.

author.name: Ivy Nguyen
author.bio: Teaches frontend architecture and form design.
